_readme: FRAME OVERRIDES — local control over every frame: transitions, elements, text.
This is the LAST layer applied. Precedence, lowest to highest:
1. public/animation/animation.json   the Animation Guider's spec (from/to, easing, idle, orbit)
2. public/animation/audio-cues.json  narration cues — overrides `start` only
3. THIS FILE                         overrides anything, and always wins
It exists so a frame can be corrected HERE, in minutes, without waiting for a
Claude Design re-export — and, critically, so those corrections SURVIVE the next
export. The SVGs are overwritten wholesale each round; this file is not.
Since mp_v2 the ids carry no meaning (art_1, scene_7, XMLID_0000006361...), so
reading the SVG tells you nothing. Run:
node scripts/inspect-frame.js frame_9
It writes frame-inspector/frame_9.inspect.svg — open it in a browser and every
targetable element is boxed and labelled with the exact id to paste below.
frame-inspector/INDEX.txt is the same thing as a plain list with positions.
Blue boxes are elements; red boxes are text (addressed text_0, text_1, … in
document order, duplicates removed).
at      number   seconds from SCENE start when it enters (overrides cue+spec)
dur     number   ms the entrance takes
x, y    number   px offset on top of the element's own placement
scale   number   multiplier, 1 = unchanged
rotate  number   degrees
opacity number   final opacity, 0-1
hide    boolean  true removes it entirely (artwork you do not want)
idle    object   { amp, period, rot } gentle loop after it settles; false stops it
orbit   object   { period, deg } continuous rotation; false stops it
cps     number   typing speed in characters/sec (default 14, capped at 2.5s total)
type    false    show the text whole, no typing
caret   false    type it, but without the cursor
"_frame": { x, y, scale }   nudge / resize the WHOLE composition
"_transition": {
inFrom:   "right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom" | "none"   (default right)
outTo:    "left"  | "right"| "top" | "bottom" | "none"   (default left)
distance: number px travelled          (default 300 in / 260 out)
frames:   number frames per half       (default TRANSITION_FRAMES = 30)
"none" holds position and lets opacity carry it — right for two scenes meant
to read as one continuous board, or a stat that should not appear to move.
─── EXAMPLE (this is a live entry, not a comment) ──────────────────────────
"frame_9.svg": {
"_transition": { "inFrom": "bottom", "frames": 24 },
"compass":     { "orbit": { "period": 26000, "deg": 360 } },
"h1":          { "hide": true }
AFTER EDITING: node scripts/audit-overrides.js  (also in the QA gate) — an
override naming a renamed id does NOTHING and says nothing, which is how this
project has lost work on every export so far.
